{"query":"Meta Louisiana data center total investment amount AI infrastructure expansion","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1439,"verdict":null,"extra_info":{"type":"featured_snippet","text":"Meta says it is expanding Louisiana data center investment to $50 billion.3 days ago"},"results":[{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/13/meta-louisiana-data-center-investment-reaches-50-billion-amid-ai-push.html","title":"Meta Louisiana data center investment reaches $50 billion amid AI push","content":"That's higher than the $27 billion figure that was revealed in October, when Meta and Blue Owl Capital formed a joint venture to help with the build-out and management of the facility.
Meta's 5-gigawatt Hyperion data center under construction in Richland Parish, Louisiana, Jan.
Jeff Landry signed into law a 20-year sales tax exemption for data centers built before 2029 as part of an effort to court Meta in the state, CNBC previously reported.
"I'm a business guy," Landry told CNBC in an interview last year.
The announcement comes after Meta had its best week on the stock market since early 2024 following the release of two major artificial intelligence models under the leadership of AI chief Alexandr Wang, head of Meta Superintelligence Labs.
"With this expansion, we will be investing over $1 billion in local infrastructure improvements, including roads, water and wastewater systems," Meta said in the post.","date":"5 days ago"},{"url":"https://www.foxbusiness.com/markets/meta-expands-louisiana-data-center-50b-ai-push-boosting-rural-community","title":"Meta expands Louisiana data center to 5 gigawatts in AI infrastructure push | Fox Business","content":"Wall Street analyst Mike Lee explains how artificial intelligence is driving the market's strong earnings story, with estimates for Q1 climbing significantly.
Meta is expanding its massive data center project in Richland Parish, Louisiana, to 5 gigawatts of compute capacity, making it one of the largest data centers in history, the company announced Monday.
The expansion pushes Meta’s total investment in the region to more than $50 billion, marking one of the biggest AI infrastructure investments in the world, according to the company.
Meta said the expansion includes an energy agreement expected to save Entergy Louisiana customers more than $2 billion over 20 years.
Teachers in the parish recently received annual bonuses of more than $50,000, up from $10,000 last year, thanks to increased tax revenue tied to the data center.","date":"5 days ago"},{"url":"https://fortune.com/2026/07/13/meta-hyperion-louisiana-50-billion-tax-breaks-locals/","title":"Meta's AI data center cost went from $10 billion to $50 billion—and split the town in two | Fortune","content":"Meta’s AI data center cost went from $10 billion to $50 billion in under 2 years—and split the town in two
That’s a huge amount of money one recipient called “jaw-dropping,” considering the average teacher salary in Louisiana is $56,785, ranking them No.
As Meta races ahead with its multi-hundred-billion-dollar AI push, rivals Microsoft, Alphabet, and Amazon are also chasing the same tax breaks and energy deals from states scrambling for a piece of the AI boom.","date":"4 days ago"},{"url":"https://www.wsj.com/tech/meta-lifts-cost-of-louisiana-data-center-to-50-billion-02b2eb25","title":"Meta Lifts Cost of Louisiana Data Center to $50 Billion - WSJ","content":"Meta Platforms META 2.78%increase; up pointing triangle has scaled up the size of its massive data-center project in Northeast Louisiana to 5 gigawatts of compute capacity and said it would now cost more than $50 billion.
The project in the rural Richland Parish region, originally pegged at a cost of $27 billion, will be the company’s largest data center.
Private-credit manager Blue Owl Capital OWL 1.14%increase; up pointing triangle owns a major stake in the project, which has attracted investors including BlackRock, according to The Wall Street Journal.
In March, it reached a deal with Entergy Louisiana to fund the energy infrastructure of the project."},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/business/meta-expands-louisiana-data-center-5-gigawatts-compute-capacity-2026-07-13/","title":"Meta expands Louisiana data center to 5 gigawatts ...","date":"4 days ago","content":"4 days ago — Meta expands Louisiana data center to 5 gigawatts, investment crosses $50 billion · Suggested Topics: · Artificial Intelligence · Water Management.Read more"}]}

Meta is expanding its massive data center project in Richland Parish, Louisiana, to 5 gigawatts of compute capacity, making it one of the largest data centers in history, the company announced Monday. The expansion pushes Meta’s total investment in the region to more than $50 billion, marking one of the biggest AI infrastructure investments in the world, according to the company. Once operational, the data center is expected to support more than 1,000 jobs. Meta said Louisiana businesses have already received more than $1.6 billion in contracts from the company since it broke ground on the site in December 2024. The tech giant also plans to spend more than $1 billion on local infrastructure upgrades, including roads, water and wastewater systems. The company said it will cover the data center’s energy, water and infrastructure costs.","score":0.942385,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://nationalcioreview.com/articles-insights/extra-bytes/meta-expands-louisiana-ai-data-center-to-more-than-50-billion","title":"Meta Expands Louisiana AI Data Center to More Than $50 Billion - The National CIO Review","content":"Meta, AI, Data Center, Expansion, Louisiana. Meta is dramatically expanding its Hyperion AI campus in Richland Parish, Louisiana, raising the project’s announced investment to more than $50 billion while growing its planned computing capacity from 2 gigawatts to 5 gigawatts. The facility is expected to become Meta’s largest AI data center and one of the world’s biggest AI superclusters, supporting the company’s next generation of AI models and AI-powered services. Access to **computing power** and reliable energy is becoming a major factor in how quickly new AI capabilities can be developed and deployed. * **A Massive AI Infrastructure Expansion**: Hyperion’s announced cost has grown from an initial $10 billion to more than $50 billion, while planned computing capacity has expanded from 2 gigawatts to 5 gigawatts. The expansion follows a series of major **AI infrastructure** commitments from Meta as the company continues building capacity for future AI development. Meta is more than doubling its Louisiana AI data center to 5 gigawatts and $50 billion – Quartz.","score":0.93666285,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.klax-tv.com/news/state/meta-expands-ai-data-center-in-louisiana-boosting-investment-to-over-50-billion/article_8103dc40-7f7a-5433-8177-c7ca7a3d44f3.html","title":"Meta expands AI data center in Louisiana, boosting investment to over $50 billion | State News | klax-tv.com","content":"Meta Data Center in Richland Parish.jpg. # Meta expands AI data center in Louisiana, boosting investment to over $50 billion. — Meta announced it is expanding its artificial intelligence-optimized data center in Richland Parish to 5 gigawatts in compute capacity, making it the largest facility in the company's fleet. The project is projected to support more than 7,500 jobs at peak construction and 1,000 operational roles once completed. Meta has already contracted more than $1.6 billion with local Louisiana businesses for infrastructure and construction projects. In response to Winter Storm Fern earlier this year, Meta and its contractors mobilized to house hundreds of utility linemen and distributed food vouchers and 1,600 lunches to local families in partnership with the Richland Parish Chamber of Commerce and Cool Kids Inc. On the sustainability front, Meta's agreement with Entergy will fund seven new natural gas-fueled generating plants, three grid-scale batteries, nuclear uprates, and other purchased power.","score":0.9224337,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://opendatascience.com/meta-expands-louisiana-ai-data-center-investment-beyond-50-billion","title":"Meta Expands Louisiana AI Data Center Investment Beyond $50 Billion - Open Data Science - Your News Source for AI, Machine Learning & more","content":"AI and Data Science Newsposted by ODSC Team  July 13, 2026 ODSC Team. The company now expects its Hyperion data center supercluster to reach 5 gigawatts of capacity and cost more than $50 billion, making it Meta’s largest data center project to date. According to CNBC, the revised investment represents a significant increase from the $27 billion figure disclosed in October, when Meta and Blue Owl Capital created a joint venture to support the facility’s construction and management. The project was initially designed as a 2GW data center. Meta expects Hyperion to reach 2GW by 2030. Meta said it will cover the full cost of the energy, water, and related infrastructure used by the facility. For data scientists and AI engineers, Hyperion highlights a shift in the industry’s competitive landscape. Meta’s Hyperion expansion shows that the next phase of AI will depend on more than models alone.
